volumeTitle: Amphitryon. The Comedy of Asses. The Pot of Gold. The Two Bacchises. The Captives
label: The Captives (Section 1.2.174b - Section 2.2.259)

sections.label: Section 1.2.174b
sections.original: Quia mi est natalis dies ;
sections.translation: Because this is my birthday ;

sections.label: Section 1.2.175
sections.original: propterea te vocari ad te ad cenam volo .
sections.translation: for that reason I ’ d like you to be invited to dinner at my house .

sections.label: Section 1.2.176
sections.original: Facete dictum . sed si pauxillo potes
sections.translation: ’ Tis kindly said .

sections.label: Section 1.2.177
sections.original: contentus esse .
sections.translation: But if you can be content to eat a very little — [*]

sections.label: Section 1.2.177b
sections.original: Ne perpauxillum modo ,
sections.translation: Aye , even ever so little ; for on such fare as that do I enjoy myself every day at home .

sections.label: Section 1.2.179
sections.original: age sis , roga emptum : nisi qui meliorem adferet
sections.translation: I ’ ll put myself up for purchase , just like a landed estate , unless any one shall privately make a better offer that pleases myself and my friends more , and to my own conditions will I bind myself .

sections.label: Section 1.2.182
sections.original: Profundum vendis tu quidem , haud fundum , mihi .
sections.translation: You are surely selling me a bottomless pit [*] , and not a landed estate . But if you are coming , do so in time .

sections.label: Section 1.2.183b
sections.original: Em , vel iam otium est .
sections.translation: Why , for that matter , I ’ m at leisure even now .

sections.label: Section 1.2.184
sections.original: I modo , venare leporem : nunc irim tenes ;
sections.translation: Go then , and hunt for a hare ; at present , in me you have but a ferret [*] ,

sections.label: Section 1.2.185
sections.original: nam meus scruposam victus commetat viam .
sections.translation: for my fare is in the way of frequenting a rugged road .

sections.label: Section 1.2.186
sections.original: Numquam istoc vinces me , Hegio , ne postules :
sections.translation: You ’ ll never repulse me by that , Hegio , so don ’ t attempt it . I ’ ll come , in spite of it , with teeth well shod .

sections.label: Section 1.2.188
sections.original: Asper meus victus sane est .
sections.translation: Really , my viands are but of a rough sort [*] .

sections.label: Section 1.2.188b
sections.original: Sentisne essitas ?
sections.translation: Are you in the habit of eating brambles ?

sections.label: Section 1.2.189
sections.original: Terrestris cena est .
sections.translation: Mine is an earthy dinner .

sections.label: Section 1.2.189b
sections.original: Sus terrestris bestia est .
sections.translation: A pig is an earthy animal .

sections.label: Section 1.2.190
sections.original: Multis holeribus .
sections.translation: Earthy from its plenty of vegetables .

sections.label: Section 1.2.190b
sections.original: Curato aegrotos domi .
sections.translation: Treat your sick peoples [*] at home with that fare ? Do you wish anything else ?

sections.label: Section 1.2.191b
sections.original: Venias temperi .
sections.translation: Come in good time .

sections.label: Section 1.2.191c
sections.original: Memorem mones .—
sections.translation: You are putting in mind one who remembers quite well .

sections.label: Section 1.2.192
sections.original: Ibo intro atque intus subducam ratiunculam ,
sections.translation: I ’ ll go in-doors , and in the house I ’ ll make the calculation how little money I have at my banker ’ s ; afterwards I ’ ll go to my brother ’ s , whither I was saying I would go .

sections.label: Section 2.1.195
sections.original: Si di ímmortales íd voluerunt , vós hanc aerumnam éxsequi ,
sections.translation: If the immortal Gods have so willed it that you should undergo this affliction , it becomes you to endure it with equanimity ; if you do so , your trouble will be lighter [*] . At home you were free men , I suppose ; now if slavery has befallen you , ‘ tis a becoming way for you to put up with it , and by your dispositions to render it light , under a master ’ s rule .

sections.label: Section 2.1.200
sections.original: indigna digna habenda sunt , erus quae facit .
sections.translation: Unworthy actions which a master does must be deemed worthy ones .

sections.label: Section 2.1.200a
sections.original: Oh oh oh .
sections.translation: Alas ! alas ! alas !

sections.label: Section 2.1.201
sections.original: Éiulatione haud opus est , oculis haud lacrimantibus :
sections.translation: There ’ s no need for wailing ; you cause much injury to your eyes . In adversity , if you use fortitude of mind , it is of service .

sections.label: Section 2.1.203
sections.original: At nós pudet , quia cúm catenis súmus .
sections.translation: But we are ashamed , because we are in bonds .

sections.label: Section 2.1.203b
sections.original: At pigeat póstea
sections.translation: But in the result it might cause vexation to our master , if he were to release you from chains ,

sections.label: Section 2.1.205
sections.original: aút solutós sinat , quós argento émerit .
sections.translation: or allow you to be loose , whom he has purchased with his money .

sections.label: Section 2.1.206
sections.original: Quid a nóbis metuit ? scímus nos
sections.translation: What does he fear from us ? We know our duty , what it is , if he allows us to be loose .

sections.label: Section 2.1.208
sections.original: Át fugam fíngitis : séntio quam rem ágitis .
sections.translation: Why , you are meditating escape . I know what it is you are devising .

sections.label: Section 2.1.209
sections.original: Nós fugiamus ? quó fugiamus ?
sections.translation: We , make our escape ? Whither should we escape ?

sections.label: Section 2.1.209b
sections.original: In patriam .
sections.translation: To your own country .

sections.label: Section 2.1.209c
sections.original: Apage , haud nós id deceat ,
sections.translation: Out upon you ; it would ill befit us to be following the example of runaways .

sections.label: Section 2.1.210b
sections.original: Immo edepol , si érit occasio , haúd dehortor .
sections.translation: Why , faith , should there be an opportunity , I don ’ t advise you not .

sections.label: Section 2.1.211
sections.original: Únum exoráre vos sínite nos .
sections.translation: Do you allow us to make one request .

sections.label: Section 2.1.211b
sections.original: Quídnam id est ?
sections.translation: What is it , pray ?

sections.label: Section 2.1.212
sections.original: Út sine hisce arbitris
sections.translation: That you will give us an opportunity of conversing , without these and yourselves for overlookers .

sections.label: Section 2.1.214
sections.original: Fíat . abscédite hinc : nós concedámus huc .
sections.translation: Be it so ; go you away from here , you people . Let ’ s step here , on one side .

sections.label: Section 2.1.215
sections.original: séd brevem orátionem íncipisse .
sections.translation: But commence upon a short conversation only .

sections.label: Section 2.1.215a
sections.original: Em istúc mihi certum erat . cóncede huc .
sections.translation: O yes , it was my intention so to do . Step aside this way .

sections.label: Section 2.1.216
sections.original: Abíte ab ístis .
sections.translation: Stand apart from them .

sections.label: Section 2.1.216b
sections.original: Obnóxii ámbo
sections.translation: We are both greatly obliged to you , by reason of your doing so , since you allow us to obtain what we are desirous of .

sections.label: Section 2.1.219
sections.original: Sécede huc núnciam , sí videtúr , procul ,
sections.translation: Step here then , at a distance now , if you think fit ,

sections.label: Section 2.1.220
sections.original: ne arbitri dicta nostra arbitrari queant
sections.translation: that no listeners may be enabled to overhear our discourse , and that this plan of ours mayn ’ t be divulged before them for a stratagem is no stratagem , if you don ’ t plan it with art but it is a very great misfortune if it becomes disclosed . For if you are my master , and I represent myself as your servant ,

sections.label: Section 2.1.225
sections.original: tamen víso opust , cauto est opus , ut hoc sóbrie sineque árbitris
sections.translation: still there ’ s need of foresight , and need of caution , that this may be carried out discreetly and without overlookers , with carefulness and with cautious prudence and diligence . So great is the mutter that has been commenced upon ; this must not be carried out in any drowsy fashion .

sections.label: Section 2.1.228b
sections.original: Ero út me volés esse .
sections.translation: Just as you shall desire me to be , I will be .

sections.label: Section 2.1.228c
sections.original: Spéro .
sections.translation: I trust so .

sections.label: Section 2.1.229
sections.original: Nam tú nunc vidés pro tuó caro cápite
sections.translation: For now you see that for your precious life I ’ m setting at stake my own , as dear to me .

sections.label: Section 2.1.231
sections.original: Scio .
sections.translation: I know it .

sections.label: Section 2.1.231b
sections.original: At scíre memento , quándo id quód voles habébis ;
sections.translation: But remember to know it when you shall be enjoying that which you wish for ; for mostly , the greatest part of mankind follow this fashion ; what they wish for , until they obtain it , they are rightminded ; but when they have now got it in their power ,

sections.label: Section 2.1.235
sections.original: éx bonis péssimi et fraúdulentíssimi
sections.translation: from being rightminded they become most deceitful , and most dishonest ; now I do consider that you are towards me as I wish . What I advise you , I would advise my own father .

sections.label: Section 2.1.237
sections.original: Pól ego si te aúdeam , meúm patrem nóminem :
sections.translation: I ’ faith , if I could venture , I would call you father ; for next to my own father , you are my nearest father .

sections.label: Section 2.1.240
sections.original: Aúdio .
sections.translation: I understand .

sections.label: Section 2.1.240b
sections.original: Et proptérea saepiús te uti memíneris moneo :
sections.translation: And therefore I remind you the more frequently , that you may remember it . I am not your master , but your servant ; now this one thing I do beseech you . Inasmuch as the immortal Gods have disclosed to us their wishes , that they desire me to have once been your master , and now to be your fellow-captive ; what formerly of my right I used to command you , now with entreaties do I beg of you ,

sections.label: Section 2.1.245
sections.original: per fortunam incertam et per mei te erga bonitatem patris ,
sections.translation: by our uncertain fortunes , and by the kindness of my father towards you , and by our common captivity , which has befallen us by the hand of the enemy , don ’ t you pay me any greater respect than I did you when you were my slave ; and don ’ t you forget to remember who you were , and who you now are .

sections.label: Section 2.1.249
sections.original: Scio quidem me te esse nunc et te esse me .
sections.translation: I know , indeed , that I now am you , and that you are I .

sections.label: Section 2.1.249b
sections.original: Em istuc si potes
sections.translation: Well , if you are able carefully to remember that , 1 have some hope in this scheme of ours .

sections.label: Section 2.2.251
sections.original: Iam ego revertar intro , si ex his quae volo exquisivero .
sections.translation: I shall return in-doors just now , when I shall have discovered from these people what I want to know . ( To the SLAVES .) Where are those persons whom I ordered to be brought out of doors here , before the house ?

sections.label: Section 2.2.253
sections.original: Edepol tibi ne in quaestione essemus cautum intellego ,
sections.translation: By my faith , I find that you have taken due precaution that we shouldn ’ t be missed by you , so walled in are we with chains and keepers .

sections.label: Section 2.2.255
sections.original: Qui cavet ne decipiatur , vix cavet , cum etiam cavet ;
sections.translation: He that takes precaution that he mayn ’ t be deceived , is hardly on his guard , even while he ’ s taking precaution ; even when he has supposed that he has taken every precaution , full often is this wary man outwitted . Was there not good reason , indeed , for me to watch you carefully , whom I purchased with so large a sum of ready money ?

sections.label: Section 2.2.259
sections.original: Neque pol tibi nos , quia nos servas , aequomst vitio vortere ,
sections.translation: Troth , it isn ’ t fair for us to hold you to blame , because you watch us closely ;
